user_query:
Hi! I’m from Ibb and I send a weekly community update to our diaspora group. Please build a small, runnable script that generates a weekly update pack from the provided local data, then run it to produce the outputs.

Inputs (use only these local files):
- input/businesses_week_prev.csv: last week’s business listings
- input/businesses_week_curr.csv: this week’s business listings
- input/events.html: a local HTML page with a table of upcoming events (table id="events")
- input/community_contacts.json: basic contact and hometown info for personalizing messages

Requirements:
1) Implement a script (e.g., Python) at scripts/generate_update.py that:
   - Parses both CSVs and computes a clear business diff:
     • New: present in current but not in previous (by id)
     • Removed: present in previous but not in current
     • Status changes: open ↔ temporarily_closed (split into Closures for open→temporarily_closed and Reopenings for temporarily_closed→open)
     • Other field updates (e.g., phone or street changed) detected by comparing records with the same id
   - Extracts upcoming events from the HTML table with id="events" into structured rows (date, title, venue, organizer)
   - Personalizes outputs using input/community_contacts.json (use hometown and names/groups to address the audience)
   - Produces all of the following artifacts under output/:
     • output/report/weekly_update.md: a concise report that mentions Ibb, and includes:
       - Summary counts: total businesses (current), new, removed, closures, reopenings, and other updates
       - Lists for each category with minimal fields:
         New: id, name, category, neighborhood
         Removed: id, name
         Closures: id, name
         Reopenings: id, name
         Other updates: id, name, fields_changed (comma-separated)
       - A section summarizing upcoming events with each event’s date, title, and venue
     • output/messages/email_draft.txt: a brief email draft to the mailing list. First line must start with "Subject:" and include "Ibb Weekly Update" and the computed counts for new and closures in the subject. The body should greet the group, summarize the counts, list the event titles with dates, and reference the local path to output/report/weekly_update.md
     • output/messages/whatsapp_message.txt: a short message (<= 500 characters) addressed to the WhatsApp group from the JSON, highlighting Ibb with the counts and at least two event titles/dates
     • output/structured/business_diff.json: machine-readable diff with keys: counts (object with totals), new (list), removed (list), closures (list), reopenings (list), field_updates (list), where each list item includes at least id and name; field_updates items must include a list of changed fields
     • output/run_log.txt: capture the exact command used to run the script on the first line, followed by a brief stdout summary from the run (e.g., generated file paths and key counts)

2) The script must be runnable from the command line with arguments, for example:
   python scripts/generate_update.py --prev input/businesses_week_prev.csv --curr input/businesses_week_curr.csv --events input/events.html --contacts input/community_contacts.json --out-dir output

3) Actually run your script once so that all output files above are created with content consistent with the inputs. Do not use the Internet; rely only on the provided files.

Deliverables to verify:
- scripts/generate_update.py (the script you created)
- All files under output/ as specified above, with content matching the provided inputs and the computed diff/events. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
